description: Known hallucinated package names from real coding-agent sessions, supplemented with research-documented patterns. Use to benchmark agent hallucination rates with vs without DepScope. Updated daily.

schema.source: observed (real agent traffic) | research (public literature) | pattern (algorithmic)
schema.evidence: short prose describing why this entry was added
schema.hit_count: how many times our API saw a 404 for this name
schema.likely_real_alternative: the actual package an agent probably meant

attribution: Rubino, V. (2026). DepScope hallucinations dataset. depscope.dev

}
```", "use_case": "BPF program loader"}

    sink   <- CZip.deflate >-> someSink -- Replace with you", "use_case": "Conduit streaming zip"}

openApiSpec = toOpenAp", "use_case": "Servant OpenAPI generation"}

3. Serialize the `OpenAPI` to YAML or JSON for documentation.

```hask", "use_case": "Servant OpenAPI generation"}

    print(key)

  run lambda { |env| [200, {'Content-Type' => 'text/plain'}, ['Hello, World!']] }
end

run app
```

This example sets up a simple Rails application with rate limiting, allowing only 100 re", "use_case": "Rack rate limiting"}
